Grants to States for Low-Income Housing Projects in Lieu of Low-Income Housing Credit Allocations for 2009, , , provided that:Pub. L. 111–5, div. B, title I, § 1602123 Stat. 362Feb. 17, 2009“(a).—In GeneralThe Secretary of the Treasury shall make a grant to the housing credit agency of each State in an amount equal to such State’s low-income housing grant election amount.“(b).—Low-Income Housing Grant Election AmountFor purposes of this section, the term ‘low-income housing grant election amount’ means, with respect to any State, such amount as the State may elect which does not exceed 85 percent of the product of—“(1)the sum of—“(A)100 percent of the State housing credit ceiling for 2009 which is attributable to amounts described in clauses (i) and (iii) of section 42(h)(3)(C) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986, and“(B)40 percent of the State housing credit ceiling for 2009 which is attributable to amounts described in clauses (ii) and (iv) of such section, multiplied by“(2)10.“(c)Subawards for Low-Income Buildings.—“(1).—In generalA State housing credit agency receiving a grant under this section shall use such grant to make subawards to finance the construction or acquisition and rehabilitation of qualified low-income buildings. A subaward under this section may be made to finance a qualified low-income building with or without an allocation under section 42 of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986, except that a State housing credit agency may make subawards to finance qualified low-income buildings without an allocation only if it makes a determination that such use will increase the total funds available to the State to build and rehabilitate affordable housing. In complying with such determination requirement, a State housing credit agency shall establish a process in which applicants that are allocated credits are required to demonstrate good faith efforts to obtain investment commitments for such credits before the agency makes such subawards.“(2).—Subawards subject to same requirements as low-income housing credit allocationsAny such subaward with respect to any qualified low-income building shall be made in the same manner and shall be subject to the same limitations (including rent, income, and use restrictions on such building) as an allocation of housing credit dollar amount allocated by such State housing credit agency under section 42 of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986, except that such subawards shall not be limited by, or otherwise affect (except as provided in subsection (h)(3)(J) of such section [section 42(h)(3) has no subpar. (J)]), the State housing credit ceiling applicable to such agency.“(3).—Compliance and asset managementThe State housing credit agency shall perform asset management functions to ensure compliance with section 42 of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 and the long-term viability of buildings funded by any subaward under this section. The State housing credit agency may collect reasonable fees from a subaward recipient to cover expenses associated with the performance of its duties under this paragraph. The State housing credit agency may retain an agent or other private contractor to satisfy the requirements of this paragraph.“(4).—RecaptureThe State housing credit agency shall impose conditions or restrictions, including a requirement providing for recapture, on any subaward under this section so as to assure that the building with respect to which such subaward is made remains a qualified low-income building during the compliance period. Any such recapture shall be payable to the Secretary of the Treasury for deposit in the general fund of the Treasury and may be enforced by means of liens or such other methods as the Secretary of the Treasury determines appropriate.“(d).—Return of Unused Grant FundsAny grant funds not used to make subawards under this section before , shall be returned to the Secretary of the Treasury on such date. Any subawards returned to the State housing credit agency on or after such date shall be promptly returned to the Secretary of the Treasury. Any amounts returned to the Secretary of the Treasury under this subsection shall be deposited in the general fund of the Treasury.January 1, 2011“(e).—DefinitionsAny term used in this section which is also used in section 42 of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 shall have the same meaning for purposes of this section as when used in such section 42. Any reference in this section to the Secretary of the Treasury shall be treated as including the Secretary’s delegate.“(f).—AppropriationsThere is hereby appropriated to the Secretary of the Treasury such sums as may be necessary to carry out this section.”

A subaward under this section may be made to finance a qualified low-income building with or without an allocation under section 42 of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986, except that a State housing credit agency may make subawards to finance qualified low-income buildings without an allocation only if it makes a determination that such use will increase the total funds available to the State to build and rehabilitate affordable housing. In complying with such determination requirement, a State housing credit agency shall establish a process in which applicants that are allocated credits are required to demonstrate good faith efforts to obtain investment commitments for such credits before the agency makes such subawards.“(2).—Subawards subject to same requirements as low-income housing credit allocationsAny such subaward with respect to any qualified low-income building shall be made in the same manner and shall be subject to the same limitations (including rent, income, and use restrictions on such building) as an allocation of housing credit dollar amount allocated by such State housing credit agency under section 42 of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986, except that such subawards shall not be limited by, or otherwise affect (except as provided in subsection (h)(3)(J) of such section [section 42(h)(3) has no subpar. (J)]), the State housing credit ceiling applicable to such agency.“(3).—Compliance and asset managementThe State housing credit agency shall perform asset management functions to ensure compliance with section 42 of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 and the long-term viability of buildings funded by any subaward under this section. The State housing credit agency may collect reasonable fees from a subaward recipient to cover expenses associated with the performance of its duties under this paragraph. The State housing credit agency may retain an agent or other private contractor to satisfy the requirements of this paragraph.“(4).—RecaptureThe State housing credit agency shall impose conditions or restrictions, including a requirement providing for recapture, on any subaward under this section so as to assure that the building with respect to which such subaward is made remains a qualified low-income building during the compliance period. Any such recapture shall be payable to the Secretary of the Treasury for deposit in the general fund of the Treasury and may be enforced by means of liens or such other methods as the Secretary of the Treasury determines appropriate.“(d).—Return of Unused Grant FundsAny grant funds not used to make subawards under this section before , shall be returned to the Secretary of the Treasury on such date. Any subawards returned to the State housing credit agency on or after such date shall be promptly returned to the Secretary of the Treasury. Any amounts returned to the Secretary of the Treasury under this subsection shall be deposited in the general fund of the Treasury.January 1, 2011“(e).—DefinitionsAny term used in this section which is also used in section 42 of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 shall have the same meaning for purposes of this section as when used in such section 42. Any reference in this section to the Secretary of the Treasury shall be treated as including the Secretary’s delegate.“(f).—AppropriationsThere is hereby appropriated to the Secretary of the Treasury such sums as may be necessary to carry out this section.”

children.heading: Minimum credit rate
children.label: 3 Minimum credit rate
children.text: In the case of any new or existing building to which paragraph (2) does not apply and which is placed in service by the taxpayer after , the applicable percentage shall not be less than 4 percent.December 31, 2020

children.text: For treatment of certain rehabilitation expenditures as separate new buildings, see subsection (e).

children.text: For determination of applicable percentage for increases in qualified basis after the 1st year of the credit period, see subsection (f)(3).

children.text: For authority of housing credit agency to limit applicable percentage and qualified basis which may be taken into account under this section with respect to any building, see subsection (h)(7).

children.heading: Qualified basis; qualified low-income building
children.label: c Qualified basis; qualified low-income building
i
children.text: the applicable fraction (determined as of the close of such taxable year) of

children.label: ii
children.text: the eligible basis of such building (determined under subsection (d)(5)).

children.heading: Applicable fraction
children.label: B Applicable fraction
children.text: For purposes of subparagraph (A), the term “applicable fraction” means the smaller of the unit fraction or the floor space fraction.

children.label: i
children.text: the numerator of which is the number of low-income units in the building, and

children.label: ii
children.text: the denominator of which is the number of residential rental units (whether or not occupied) in such building.

children.label: i
children.text: the numerator of which is the total floor space of the low-income units in such building, and

children.label: ii
children.text: the denominator of which is the total floor space of the residential rental units (whether or not occupied) in such building.

children.heading: Qualified basis to include portion of building used to provide supportive services for homeless
children.label: E Qualified basis to include portion of building used to provide supportive services for homeless
i
children.text: so much of the eligible basis of such building as is used throughout the year to provide supportive services designed to assist tenants in locating and retaining permanent housing, or

children.label: ii
children.text: 20 percent of the qualified basis of such building (determined without regard to this subparagraph).

children.label: i
children.text: beginning on the 1st day in the compliance period on which such building is part of such a project, and

children.label: ii
children.text: ending on the last day of the compliance period with respect to such building, and

children.label: B
children.text: to which the amendments made by section 201(a) of the Tax Reform Act of 1986 apply.

children.heading: New buildings
children.label: 1 New buildings
children.text: The eligible basis of a new building is its adjusted basis as of the close of the 1st taxable year of the credit period.

children.label: i
children.text: in the case of a building which meets the requirements of subparagraph (B), its adjusted basis as of the close of the 1st taxable year of the credit period, and

children.label: ii
children.text: zero in any other case.

children.label: i
children.text: the building is acquired by purchase (as defined in section 179(d)(2)),

children.label: ii
children.text: there is a period of at least 10 years between the date of its acquisition by the taxpayer and the date the building was last placed in service,

children.label: iii
children.text: the building was not previously placed in service by the taxpayer or by any person who was a related person with respect to the taxpayer as of the time previously placed in service, and

children.label: iv
children.text: except as provided in subsection (f)(5), a credit is allowable under subsection (a) by reason of subsection (e) with respect to the building.

children.heading: Adjusted basis
children.label: C Adjusted basis
children.text: For purposes of subparagraph (A), the adjusted basis of any building shall not include so much of the basis of such building as is determined by reference to the basis of other property held at any time by the person acquiring the building.

children.heading: Special rules for certain transfers
children.label: i Special rules for certain transfers
I
children.text: in connection with the acquisition of the building in a transaction in which the basis of the building in the hands of the person acquiring it is determined in whole or in part by reference to the adjusted basis of such building in the hands of the person from whom acquired,

children.label: II
children.text: by a person whose basis in such building is determined under section 1014(a) (relating to property acquired from a decedent),

children.label: III
children.text: by any governmental unit or qualified nonprofit organization (as defined in subsection (h)(5)) if the requirements of subparagraph (B)(ii) are met with respect to the placement in service by such unit or organization and all the income from such property is exempt from Federal income taxation,

children.label: IV
children.text: by any person who acquired such building by foreclosure (or by instrument in lieu of foreclosure) of any purchase-money security interest held by such person if the requirements of subparagraph (B)(ii) are met with respect to the placement in service by such person and such building is resold within 12 months after the date such building is placed in service by such person after such foreclosure, or

children.label: V
children.text: of a single-family residence by any individual who owned and used such residence for no other purpose than as his principal residence.

children.heading: Related person
children.label: ii Related person
children.text: For purposes of subparagraph (B)(iii), a person (hereinafter in this subclause referred to as the “related person”) is related to any person if the related person bears a relationship to such person specified in section 267(b) or 707(b)(1), or the related person and such person are engaged in trades or businesses under common control (within the meaning of subsections (a) and (b) of section 52).

children.heading: Eligible basis reduced where disproportionate standards for units
children.label: 3 Eligible basis reduced where disproportionate standards for units

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Except as provided in subparagraph (B), the eligible basis of any building shall be reduced by an amount equal to the portion of the adjusted basis of the building which is attributable to residential rental units in the building which are not low-income units and which are above the average quality standard of the low-income units in the building.

children.heading: Exception where taxpayer elects to exclude excess costs
children.label: B Exception where taxpayer elects to exclude excess costs
I
children.text: the excess described in clause (ii) with respect to such unit is not greater than 15 percent of the cost described in clause (ii)(II), and

children.label: II
children.text: the taxpayer elects to exclude from the eligible basis of such building the excess described in clause (ii) with respect to such unit.

children.label: I
children.text: the cost of such unit, over

children.label: II
children.text: the amount which would be the cost of such unit if the average cost per square foot of low-income units in the building were substituted for the cost per square foot of such unit.

children.heading: Special rules relating to determination of adjusted basis
children.label: 4 Special rules relating to determination of adjusted basis

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Except as provided in subparagraphs (B) and (C), the adjusted basis of any building shall be determined without regard to the adjusted basis of any property which is not residential rental property.

children.heading: Basis of property in common areas, etc., included
children.label: B Basis of property in common areas, etc., included
children.text: The adjusted basis of any building shall be determined by taking into account the adjusted basis of property (of a character subject to the allowance for depreciation) used in common areas or provided as comparable amenities to all residential rental units in such building.

children.heading: Inclusion of basis of property used to provide services for certain nontenants
children.label: C Inclusion of basis of property used to provide services for certain nontenants

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The adjusted basis of any building located in a qualified census tract (as defined in paragraph (5)(B)(ii)) shall be determined by taking into account the adjusted basis of property (of a character subject to the allowance for depreciation and not otherwise taken into account) used throughout the taxable year in providing any community service facility.

children.label: I
children.text: 25 percent of so much of the eligible basis of the qualified low-income housing project of which it is a part as does not exceed $15,000,000, plus

children.label: II
children.text: 10 percent of so much of the eligible basis of such project as is not taken into account under subclause (I).

children.heading: Community service facility
children.label: iii Community service facility
children.text: For purposes of this subparagraph, the term “community service facility” means any facility designed to serve primarily individuals whose income is 60 percent or less of area median income (within the meaning of subsection (g)(1)(B)).

children.heading: No reduction for depreciation
children.label: D No reduction for depreciation
children.text: The adjusted basis of any building shall be determined without regard to paragraphs (2) and (3) of section 1016(a).

children.heading: Special rules for determining eligible basis
children.label: 5 Special rules for determining eligible basis

children.heading: Federal grants not taken into account in determining eligible basis
children.label: A Federal grants not taken into account in determining eligible basis
children.text: The eligible basis of a building shall not include any costs financed with the proceeds of a federally funded grant.

children.heading: Increase in credit for buildings in high cost areas
children.label: B Increase in credit for buildings in high cost areas
I
children.text: in the case of a new building, the eligible basis of such building shall be 130 percent of such basis determined without regard to this subparagraph, and

children.label: II
children.text: in the case of an existing building, the rehabilitation expenditures taken into account under subsection (e) shall be 130 percent of such expenditures determined without regard to this subparagraph.

children.heading: In general
children.label: I In general
children.text: The term “qualified census tract” means any census tract which is designated by the Secretary of Housing and Urban Development and, for the most recent year for which census data are available on household income in such tract, either in which 50 percent or more of the households have an income which is less than 60 percent of the area median gross income for such year or which has a poverty rate of at least 25 percent. If the Secretary of Housing and Urban Development determines that sufficient data for any period are not available to apply this clause on the basis of census tracts, such Secretary shall apply this clause for such period on the basis of enumeration districts.

children.heading: Limit on MSA’s designated
children.label: II Limit on MSA’s designated
children.text: The portion of a metropolitan statistical area which may be designated for purposes of this subparagraph shall not exceed an area having 20 percent of the population of such metropolitan statistical area.

children.heading: Determination of areas
children.label: III Determination of areas
children.text: For purposes of this clause, each metropolitan statistical area shall be treated as a separate area and all nonmetropolitan areas in a State shall be treated as 1 area.

children.heading: In general
children.label: I In general
children.text: The term “difficult development areas” means any area designated by the Secretary of Housing and Urban Development as an area which has high construction, land, and utility costs relative to area median gross income.

children.heading: Limit on areas designated
children.label: II Limit on areas designated
children.text: The portions of metropolitan statistical areas which may be designated for purposes of this subparagraph shall not exceed an aggregate area having 20 percent of the population of such metropolitan statistical areas. A comparable rule shall apply to nonmetropolitan areas.

children.label: I
children.text: population shall be determined on the basis of the most recent decennial census for which data are available,

children.label: II
children.text: area median gross income shall be determined in accordance with subsection (g)(4),

children.label: III
children.text: the term “metropolitan statistical area” has the same meaning as when used in section 143(k)(2)(B), and

children.label: IV
children.text: the term “nonmetropolitan area” means any county (or portion thereof) which is not within a metropolitan statistical area.

children.heading: Buildings designated by State housing credit agency
children.label: v Buildings designated by State housing credit agency
children.text: Any building which is designated by the State housing credit agency as requiring the increase in credit under this subparagraph in order for such building to be financially feasible as part of a qualified low-income housing project shall be treated for purposes of this subparagraph as located in a difficult development area which is designated for purposes of this subparagraph. The preceding sentence shall not apply to any building if paragraph (1) of subsection (h) does not apply to any portion of the eligible basis of such building by reason of paragraph (4) of such subsection.

children.heading: Credit allowable for certain buildings acquired during 10-year period described in paragraph (2)(B)(ii)
children.label: 6 Credit allowable for certain buildings acquired during 10-year period described in paragraph (2)(B)(ii)

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Paragraph (2)(B)(ii) shall not apply to any federally- or State-assisted building.

children.heading: Buildings acquired from insured depository institutions in default
children.label: B Buildings acquired from insured depository institutions in default
children.text: On application by the taxpayer, the Secretary may waive paragraph (2)(B)(ii) with respect to any building acquired from an insured depository institution in default (as defined in section 3 of the Federal Deposit Insurance Act) or from a receiver or conservator of such an institution.

children.heading: Federally-assisted building
children.label: i Federally-assisted building
children.text: The term “federally-assisted building” means any building which is substantially assisted, financed, or operated under section 8 of the United States Housing Act of 1937, section 221(d)(3), 221(d)(4), or 236 of the National Housing Act, section 515 of the Housing Act of 1949, or any other housing program administered by the Department of Housing and Urban Development or by the Rural Housing Service of the Department of Agriculture.

children.heading: State-assisted building
children.label: ii State-assisted building
children.text: The term “State-assisted building” means any building which is substantially assisted, financed, or operated under any State law similar in purposes to any of the laws referred to in clause (i).

children.label: 7 Acquisition of building before end of prior compliance period

children.heading: Rehabilitation expenditures treated as separate new building
children.label: e Rehabilitation expenditures treated as separate new building

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Rehabilitation expenditures paid or incurred by the taxpayer with respect to any building shall be treated for purposes of this section as a separate new building.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The term “rehabilitation expenditures” means amounts chargeable to capital account and incurred for property (or additions or improvements to property) of a character subject to the allowance for depreciation in connection with the rehabilitation of a building.

children.heading: Cost of acquisition, etc., not included
children.label: B Cost of acquisition, etc., not included
children.text: Such term does not include the cost of acquiring any building (or interest therein) or any amount not permitted to be taken into account under paragraph (3) or (4) of subsection (d).

children.label: II
children.text: The requirement of this subclause is met if the qualified basis attributable to such amount, when divided by the number of low-income units in the building, is $6,000 or more.

children.heading: Exception from 10 percent rehabilitation
children.label: B Exception from 10 percent rehabilitation
children.text: In the case of a building acquired by the taxpayer from a governmental unit, at the election of the taxpayer, subparagraph (A)(ii)(I) shall not apply and the credit under this section for such rehabilitation expenditures shall be determined using the percentage applicable under subsection (b)(2)(B)(ii).

children.heading: No double counting
children.label: 5 No double counting
children.text: Rehabilitation expenditures may, at the election of the taxpayer, be taken into account under this subsection or subsection (d)(2)(A)(i) but not under both such subsections.

children.heading: Regulations to apply subsection with respect to group of units in building
children.label: 6 Regulations to apply subsection with respect to group of units in building
children.text: The Secretary may prescribe regulations, consistent with the purposes of this subsection, treating a group of units with respect to which rehabilitation expenditures are incurred as a separate new building.

children.label: B Disallowed 1st year credit allowed in 11th year
children.text: Any reduction by reason of subparagraph (A) in the credit allowable (without regard to subparagraph (A)) for the 1st taxable year of the credit period shall be allowable under subsection (a) for the 1st taxable year following the credit period.

children.text: If a building (or an interest therein) is disposed of during any year for which credit is allowable under subsection (a), such credit shall be allocated between the parties on the basis of the number of days during such year the building (or interest) was held by each. In any such case, proper adjustments shall be made in the application of subsection (j).

children.label: 5 Credit period for existing buildings not to begin before rehabilitation credit allowed

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The credit period for an existing building shall not begin before the 1st taxable year of the credit period for rehabilitation expenditures with respect to the building.

children.label: B Acquisition credit allowed for certain buildings not allowed a rehabilitation credit

children.heading: 20–50 test
children.label: A 20–50 test
children.text: The project meets the requirements of this subparagraph if 20 percent or more of the residential units in such project are both rent-restricted and occupied by individuals whose income is 50 percent or less of area median gross income.

children.heading: 40–60 test
children.label: B 40–60 test
children.text: The project meets the requirements of this subparagraph if 40 percent or more of the residential units in such project are both rent-restricted and occupied by individuals whose income is 60 percent or less of area median gross income.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The project meets the minimum requirements of this subparagraph if 40 percent or more (25 percent or more in the case of a project described in section 142(d)(6)) of the residential units in such project are both rent-restricted and occupied by individuals whose income does not exceed the imputed income limitation designated by the taxpayer with respect to the respective unit.

children.heading: Special rules relating to income limitation
children.label: ii Special rules relating to income limitation

children.heading: Designation
children.label: I Designation
children.text: The taxpayer shall designate the imputed income limitation of each unit taken into account under such clause.

children.heading: Average test
children.label: II Average test
children.text: The average of the imputed income limitations designated under subclause (I) shall not exceed 60 percent of area median gross income.

children.heading: 10-percent increments
children.label: III 10-percent increments
children.text: The designated imputed income limitation of any unit under subclause (I) shall be 20 percent, 30 percent, 40 percent, 50 percent, 60 percent, 70 percent, or 80 percent of area median gross income.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: For purposes of paragraph (1), a residential unit is rent-restricted if the gross rent with respect to such unit does not exceed 30 percent of the imputed income limitation applicable to such unit. For purposes of the preceding sentence, the amount of the income limitation under paragraph (1) applicable for any period shall not be less than such limitation applicable for the earliest period the building (which contains the unit) was included in the determination of whether the project is a qualified low-income housing project.

children.label: i

children.label: ii
children.text: includes any utility allowance determined by the Secretary after taking into account such determinations under section 8 of the United States Housing Act of 1937,

children.label: iii
children.text: does not include any fee for a supportive service which is paid to the owner of the unit (on the basis of the low-income status of the tenant of the unit) by any governmental program of assistance (or by an organization described in section 501(c)(3) and exempt from tax under section 501(a)) if such program (or organization) provides assistance for rent and the amount of assistance provided for rent is not separable from the amount of assistance provided for supportive services, and

children.text: does not include any rental payment to the owner of the unit to the extent such owner pays an equivalent amount to the Farmers’ Home Administration under section 515 of the Housing Act of 1949.

children.label: D Treatment of units occupied by individuals whose incomes rise above limit

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Except as provided in clauses (ii), (iii), and (iv), notwithstanding an increase in the income of the occupants of a low-income unit above the income limitation applicable under paragraph (1), such unit shall continue to be treated as a low-income unit if the income of such occupants initially met such income limitation and such unit continues to be rent-restricted.

children.label: ii Rental of next available unit in case of 20–50 or 40–60 test
children.text: In the case of a project with respect to which the taxpayer elects the requirements of subparagraph (A) or (B) of paragraph (1), if the income of the occupants of the unit increases above 140 percent of the income limitation applicable under paragraph (1), clause (i) shall cease to apply to such unit if any residential rental unit in the building (of a size comparable to, or smaller than, such unit) is occupied by a new resident whose income exceeds such income limitation.

children.text: Except as otherwise provided in this paragraph, a building shall be treated as a qualified low-income building only if the project (of which such building is a part) meets the requirements of paragraph (1) not later than the close of the 1st year of the credit period for such building.

children.label: i In general
children.text: In determining whether a building (hereinafter in this subparagraph referred to as the “prior building”) is a qualified low-income building, the taxpayer may take into account 1 or more additional buildings placed in service during the 12-month period described in subparagraph (A) with respect to the prior building only if the taxpayer elects to apply clause (ii) with respect to each additional building taken into account.

children.heading: Treatment of elected buildings
children.label: ii Treatment of elected buildings
children.text: In the case of a building which the taxpayer elects to take into account under clause (i), the period under subparagraph (A) for such building shall end at the close of the 12-month period applicable to the prior building.

children.heading: Date prior building is treated as placed in service
children.label: iii Date prior building is treated as placed in service
children.text: For purposes of determining the credit period and the compliance period for the prior building, the prior building shall be treated for purposes of this section as placed in service on the most recent date any additional building elected by the taxpayer (with respect to such prior building) was placed in service.

children.label: D Projects with more than 1 building must be identified
children.text: For purposes of this section, a project shall be treated as consisting of only 1 building unless, before the close of the 1st calendar year in the project period (as defined in subsection (h)(1)(F)(ii)), each building which is (or will be) part of such project is identified in such form and manner as the Secretary may provide.

children.heading: Certain rules made applicable
children.label: 4 Certain rules made applicable
children.text: Paragraphs (2) (other than subparagraph (A) thereof), (3), (4), (5), (6), and (7) of section 142(d), and section 6652(j), shall apply for purposes of determining whether any project is a qualified low-income housing project and whether any unit is a low-income unit; except that, in applying such provisions for such purposes, the term “gross rent” shall have the meaning given such term by paragraph (2)(B) of this subsection.

children.heading: Election to treat building after compliance period as not part of a project
children.label: 5 Election to treat building after compliance period as not part of a project
children.text: For purposes of this section, the taxpayer may elect to treat any building as not part of a qualified low-income housing project for any period beginning after the compliance period for such building.

children.label: 7 Scattered site projects
children.text: Buildings which would (but for their lack of proximity) be treated as a project for purposes of this section shall be so treated if all of the dwelling units in each of the buildings are rent-restricted (within the meaning of paragraph (2)) residential rental units.

children.text: The amount of the credit determined under this section for any taxable year with respect to any building shall not exceed the housing credit dollar amount allocated to such building under this subsection.

children.text: Except in the case of an allocation which meets the requirements of subparagraph (C), (D), (E), or (F), an allocation shall be taken into account under subparagraph (A) only if it is made not later than the close of the calendar year in which the building is placed in service.

children.text: An allocation meets the requirements of this subparagraph if there is a binding commitment (not later than the close of the calendar year in which the building is placed in service) by the housing credit agency to allocate a specified housing credit dollar amount to such building beginning in a specified later taxable year.

children.text: For purposes of clause (i), the term “qualified building” means any building which is part of a project if the taxpayer’s basis in such project (as of the date which is 1 year after the date that the allocation was made) is more than 10 percent of the taxpayer’s reasonably expected basis in such project (as of the close of the second calendar year referred to in clause (i)). Such term does not include any existing building unless a credit is allowable under subsection (e) for rehabilitation expenditures paid or incurred by the taxpayer with respect to such building for a taxable year ending during the second calendar year referred to in clause (i) or the prior taxable year.

children.text: For purposes of subparagraph (A), a qualified low-income housing project is described in this subparagraph if a qualified nonprofit organization is to own an interest in the project (directly or through a partnership) and materially participate (within the meaning of section 469(h)) in the development and operation of the project throughout the compliance period.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: For purposes of this paragraph, a qualified nonprofit organization shall be treated as satisfying the ownership and material participation test of subparagraph (B) if any qualified corporation in which such organization holds stock satisfies such test.

children.heading: Qualified corporation
children.label: ii Qualified corporation
children.text: For purposes of clause (i), the term “qualified corporation” means any corporation if 100 percent of the stock of such corporation is held by 1 or more qualified nonprofit organizations at all times during the period such corporation is in existence.

children.heading: State may not override set-aside
children.label: E State may not override set-aside
children.text: Nothing in subparagraph (F) of paragraph (3) shall be construed to permit a State not to comply with subparagraph (A) of this paragraph.

children.heading: Buildings eligible for credit only if minimum long-term commitment to low-income housing
children.label: 6 Buildings eligible for credit only if minimum long-term commitment to low-income housing

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: No credit shall be allowed by reason of this section with respect to any building for the taxable year unless an extended low-income housing commitment is in effect as of the end of such taxable year.

children.label: i
children.text: which requires that the applicable fraction (as defined in subsection (c)(1)) for the building for each taxable year in the extended use period will not be less than the applicable fraction specified in such agreement and which prohibits the actions described in subclauses (I) and (II) of subparagraph (E)(ii),

children.label: ii
children.text: which allows individuals who meet the income limitation applicable to the building under subsection (g) (whether prospective, present, or former occupants of the building) the right to enforce in any State court the requirement and prohibitions of clause (i),

children.label: iii
children.text: which prohibits the disposition to any person of any portion of the building to which such agreement applies unless all of the building to which such agreement applies is disposed of to such person,

children.label: iv
children.text: which prohibits the refusal to lease to a holder of a voucher or certificate of eligibility under section 8 of the United States Housing Act of 1937 because of the status of the prospective tenant as such a holder,

children.label: v
children.text: which is binding on all successors of the taxpayer, and

children.label: vi
children.text: which, with respect to the property, is recorded pursuant to State law as a restrictive covenant.

children.heading: Allocation of credit may not exceed amount necessary to support commitment
children.label: C Allocation of credit may not exceed amount necessary to support commitment

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The housing credit dollar amount allocated to any building may not exceed the amount necessary to support the applicable fraction specified in the extended low-income housing commitment for such building, including any increase in such fraction pursuant to the application of subsection (f)(3) if such increase is reflected in an amended low-income housing commitment.

children.heading: Buildings financed by tax-exempt bonds
children.label: ii Buildings financed by tax-exempt bonds
children.text: If paragraph (4) applies to any building the amount of credit allowed in any taxable year may not exceed the amount necessary to support the applicable fraction specified in the extended low-income housing commitment for such building. Such commitment may be amended to increase such fraction.

children.label: i
children.text: beginning on the 1st day in the compliance period on which such building is part of a qualified low-income housing project, and

children.label: I
children.text: the date specified by such agency in such agreement, or

children.label: II
children.text: the date which is 15 years after the close of the compliance period.

children.heading: Exceptions if foreclosure or if no buyer willing to maintain low-income status
children.label: E Exceptions if foreclosure or if no buyer willing to maintain low-income status
I
children.text: on the date the building is acquired by foreclosure (or instrument in lieu of foreclosure) unless the Secretary determines that such acquisition is part of an arrangement with the taxpayer a purpose of which is to terminate such period, or

children.label: II
children.text: on the last day of the period specified in subparagraph (I) if the housing credit agency is unable to present during such period a qualified contract for the acquisition of the low-income portion of the building by any person who will continue to operate such portion as a qualified low-income building.

children.heading: Eviction, etc. of existing low-income tenants not permitted
children.label: ii Eviction, etc. of existing low-income tenants not permitted
I
children.text: the eviction or the termination of tenancy (other than for good cause) of an existing tenant of any low-income unit, or

children.label: II
children.text: any increase in the gross rent with respect to such unit not otherwise permitted under this section.

children.label: II

children.label: ii
children.text: cash distributions from (or available for distribution from) the project.

children.label: I

children.label: II
children.text: the cost-of-living adjustment for such calendar year, determined under section 1(f)(3) by substituting the base calendar year for “calendar year 2016” in subparagraph (A)(ii) thereof.

children.heading: Cost-of-living increases in excess of 5 percent not taken into account
children.label: ii Cost-of-living increases in excess of 5 percent not taken into account
children.text: Under regulations prescribed by the Secretary, if the C-CPI-U for any calendar year (as defined in section 1(f)(6)) exceeds the C-CPI-U for the preceding calendar year by more than 5 percent, the C-CPI-U for the base calendar year shall be increased such that such excess shall never be taken into account under clause (i). In the case of a base calendar year before 2017, the C-CPI-U for such year shall be determined by multiplying the CPI for such year by the amount determined under section 1(f)(3)(B).

children.heading: Base calendar year
children.label: iii Base calendar year
children.text: For purposes of this subparagraph, the term “base calendar year” means the calendar year with or within which the 1st taxable year of the credit period ends.

children.heading: Low-income portion
children.label: H Low-income portion
children.text: For purposes of this paragraph, the low-income portion of a building is the portion of such building equal to the applicable fraction specified in the extended low-income housing commitment for the building.

children.heading: Period for finding buyer
children.label: I Period for finding buyer
children.text: The period referred to in this subparagraph is the 1-year period beginning on the date (after the 14th year of the compliance period) the taxpayer submits a written request to the housing credit agency to find a person to acquire the taxpayer’s interest in the low-income portion of the building.

children.heading: Effect of noncompliance
children.label: J Effect of noncompliance
children.text: If, during a taxable year, there is a determination that an extended low-income housing agreement was not in effect as of the beginning of such year, such determination shall not apply to any period before such year and subparagraph (A) shall be applied without regard to such determination if the failure is corrected within 1 year from the date of the determination.

children.heading: Projects which consist of more than 1 building
children.label: K Projects which consist of more than 1 building
children.text: The application of this paragraph to projects which consist of more than 1 building shall be made under regulations prescribed by the Secretary.

children.heading: Building must be located within jurisdiction of credit agency
children.label: A Building must be located within jurisdiction of credit agency
children.text: A housing credit agency may allocate its aggregate housing credit dollar amount only to buildings located in the jurisdiction of the governmental unit of which such agency is a part.

children.heading: Agency allocations in excess of limit
children.label: B Agency allocations in excess of limit
children.text: If the aggregate housing credit dollar amounts allocated by a housing credit agency for any calendar year exceed the portion of the State housing credit ceiling allocated to such agency for such calendar year, the housing credit dollar amounts so allocated shall be reduced (to the extent of such excess) for buildings in the reverse of the order in which the allocations of such amounts were made.

children.heading: Credit reduced if allocated credit dollar amount is less than credit which would be allowable without regard to placed in service convention, etc.
children.label: C Credit reduced if allocated credit dollar amount is less than credit which would be allowable without regard to placed in service convention, etc.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The amount of the credit determined under this section with respect to any building shall not exceed the clause (ii) percentage of the amount of the credit which would (but for this subparagraph) be determined under this section with respect to such building.

children.label: I
children.text: the housing credit dollar amount allocated to such building bears to

children.label: II
children.text: the credit amount determined in accordance with clause (iii).

children.label: I
children.text: this section were applied without regard to paragraphs (2)(A) and (3)(B) of subsection (f), and

children.label: II
children.text: subsection (f)(3)(A) were applied without regard to “the percentage equal to ⅔ of”.

children.heading: Housing credit agency to specify applicable percentage and maximum qualified basis
children.label: D Housing credit agency to specify applicable percentage and maximum qualified basis
children.text: In allocating a housing credit dollar amount to any building, the housing credit agency shall specify the applicable percentage and the maximum qualified basis which may be taken into account under this section with respect to such building. The applicable percentage and maximum qualified basis so specified shall not exceed the applicable percentage and qualified basis determined under this section without regard to this subsection.

children.heading: Housing credit agency
children.label: A Housing credit agency
children.text: The term “housing credit agency” means any agency authorized to carry out this subsection.

children.heading: Possessions treated as States
children.label: B Possessions treated as States
children.text: The term “State” includes a possession of the United States.

children.heading: Compliance period
children.label: 1 Compliance period
children.text: The term “compliance period” means, with respect to any building, the period of 15 taxable years beginning with the 1st taxable year of the credit period with respect thereto.

children.heading: Determination of whether building is federally subsidized
children.label: 2 Determination of whether building is federally subsidized

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Except as otherwise provided in this paragraph, for purposes of subsection (b)(1), a new building shall be treated as federally subsidized for any taxable year if, at any time during such taxable year or any prior taxable year, there is or was outstanding any obligation the interest on which is exempt from tax under section 103 the proceeds of which are or were used (directly or indirectly) with respect to such building or the operation thereof.1So in original. See 2008 Amendment note below.1

children.heading: Election to reduce eligible basis by proceeds of obligations
children.label: B Election to reduce eligible basis by proceeds of obligations
children.text: A tax-exempt obligation shall not be taken into account under subparagraph (A) if the taxpayer elects to exclude from the eligible basis of the building for purposes of subsection (d) the proceeds of such obligation.

children.heading: Special rule for subsidized construction financing
children.label: C Special rule for subsidized construction financing
i
children.text: such obligation (when issued) identified the building for which the proceeds of such obligation would be used, and

children.label: ii
children.text: such obligation is redeemed before such building is placed in service.

children.label: i
children.text: such unit is rent-restricted (as defined in subsection (g)(2)), and

children.label: ii
children.text: the individuals occupying such unit meet the income limitation applicable under subsection (g)(1) to the project of which such building is a part.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: A unit shall not be treated as a low-income unit unless the unit is suitable for occupancy and used other than on a transient basis.

children.heading: Suitability for occupancy
children.label: ii Suitability for occupancy
children.text: For purposes of clause (i), the suitability of a unit for occupancy shall be determined under regulations prescribed by the Secretary taking into account local health, safety, and building codes.

children.label: I
children.text: which is used exclusively to facilitate the transition of homeless individuals (within the meaning of section 103 of the McKinney-Vento Homeless Assistance Act (), as in effect on the date of the enactment of this clause) to independent living within 24 months, and42 U.S.C. 11302

children.label: II
children.text: in which a governmental entity or qualified nonprofit organization (as defined in subsection (h)(5)) provides such individuals with temporary housing and supportive services designed to assist such individuals in locating and retaining permanent housing.

children.heading: Single-room occupancy units
children.label: iv Single-room occupancy units
children.text: For purposes of clause (i), a single-room occupancy unit shall not be treated as used on a transient basis merely because it is rented on a month-by-month basis.

children.heading: Special rule for buildings having 4 or fewer units
children.label: C Special rule for buildings having 4 or fewer units
i
children.text: any individual who occupies a residential unit in such building, or

children.label: ii
children.text: any person who is related (as defined in subsection (d)(2)(D)(iii)) to such individual.

children.heading: Certain students not to disqualify unit
children.label: D Certain students not to disqualify unit
I
children.text: a student and receiving assistance under title IV of the Social Security Act,

children.label: II
children.text: a student who was previously under the care and placement responsibility of the State agency responsible for administering a plan under part B or part E of title IV of the Social Security Act, or

children.label: III
children.text: enrolled in a job training program receiving assistance under the Job Training Partnership Act or under other similar Federal, State, or local laws, or

children.label: I
children.text: single parents and their children and such parents are not dependents (as defined in section 152, determined without regard to subsections (b)(1), (b)(2), and (d)(1)(B) thereof) of another individual and such children are not dependents (as so defined) of another individual other than a parent of such children, or

children.label: II
children.text: married and file a joint return.

children.heading: Owner-occupied buildings having 4 or fewer units eligible for credit where development plan
children.label: E Owner-occupied buildings having 4 or fewer units eligible for credit where development plan

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Subparagraph (C) shall not apply to the acquisition or rehabilitation of a building pursuant to a development plan of action sponsored by a State or local government or a qualified nonprofit organization (as defined in subsection (h)(5)(C)).

children.heading: Limitation on credit
children.label: ii Limitation on credit
children.text: In the case of a building to which clause (i) applies, the applicable fraction shall not exceed 80 percent of the unit fraction.

children.heading: Certain unrented units treated as owner-occupied
children.label: iii Certain unrented units treated as owner-occupied
children.text: In the case of a building to which clause (i) applies, any unit which is not rented for 90 days or more shall be treated as occupied by the owner of the building as of the 1st day it is not rented.

children.heading: New building
children.label: 4 New building
children.text: The term “new building” means a building the original use of which begins with the taxpayer.

children.heading: Existing building
children.label: 5 Existing building
children.text: The term “existing building” means any building which is not a new building.

children.heading: Application to estates and trusts
children.label: 6 Application to estates and trusts
children.text: In the case of an estate or trust, the amount of the credit determined under subsection (a) and any increase in tax under subsection (j) shall be apportioned between the estate or trust and the beneficiaries on the basis of the income of the estate or trust allocable to each.

children.heading: Impact of tenant’s right of 1st refusal to acquire property
children.label: 7 Impact of tenant’s right of 1st refusal to acquire property

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: No Federal income tax benefit shall fail to be allowable to the taxpayer with respect to any qualified low-income building merely by reason of a right of 1st refusal held by the tenants (in cooperative form or otherwise) or resident management corporation of such building or by a qualified nonprofit organization (as defined in subsection (h)(5)(C)) or government agency to purchase the property after the close of the compliance period for a price which is not less than the minimum purchase price determined under subparagraph (B).

children.label: i
children.text: the principal amount of outstanding indebtedness secured by the building (other than indebtedness incurred within the 5-year period ending on the date of the sale to the tenants), and

children.label: ii
children.text: all Federal, State, and local taxes attributable to such sale.

children.heading: Treatment of rural projects
children.label: 8 Treatment of rural projects
children.text: For purposes of this section, in the case of any project for residential rental property located in a rural area (as defined in section 520 of the Housing Act of 1949), any income limitation measured by reference to area median gross income shall be measured by reference to the greater of area median gross income or national non-metropolitan median income. The preceding sentence shall not apply with respect to any building if paragraph (1) of section 42(h) does not apply by reason of paragraph (4) thereof to any portion of the credit determined under this section with respect to such building.

children.heading: Reduction in State housing credit ceiling for low-income housing grants received in 2009
children.label: A Reduction in State housing credit ceiling for low-income housing grants received in 2009
children.text: For purposes of this section, the amounts described in clauses (i) through (iv) of subsection (h)(3)(C) with respect to any State for 2009 shall each be reduced by so much of such amount as is taken into account in determining the amount of any grant to such State under section 1602 of the American Recovery and Reinvestment Tax Act of 2009.

children.heading: Special rule for basis
children.label: B Special rule for basis
children.text: Basis of a qualified low-income building shall not be reduced by the amount of any grant described in subparagraph (A).

children.label: B

children.label: A
children.text: the aggregate decrease in the credits allowed to the taxpayer under section 38 for all prior taxable years which would have resulted if the accelerated portion of the credit allowable by reason of this section were not allowed for all prior taxable years with respect to the excess of the amount described in paragraph (1)(B) over the amount described in paragraph (1)(A), plus

children.label: B
children.text: interest at the overpayment rate established under section 6621 on the amount determined under subparagraph (A) for each prior taxable year for the period beginning on the due date for filing the return for the prior taxable year involved.

children.label: A

children.label: B
children.text: the aggregate credit which would be allowable by reason of this section for such years with respect to such basis if the aggregate credit which would (but for this subsection) have been allowable for the entire compliance period were allowable ratably over 15 years.

children.heading: Tax benefit rule
children.label: A Tax benefit rule
children.text: The tax for the taxable year shall be increased under paragraph (1) only with respect to credits allowed by reason of this section which were used to reduce tax liability. In the case of credits not so used to reduce tax liability, the carryforwards and carrybacks under section 39 shall be appropriately adjusted.

children.heading: Only basis for which credit allowed taken into account
children.label: B Only basis for which credit allowed taken into account
children.text: Qualified basis shall be taken into account under paragraph (1)(B) only to the extent such basis was taken into account in determining the credit under subsection (a) for the preceding taxable year referred to in such paragraph.

children.heading: No recapture of additional credit allowable by reason of subsection (f)(3)
children.label: C No recapture of additional credit allowable by reason of subsection (f)(3)
children.text: Paragraph (1) shall apply to a decrease in qualified basis only to the extent such decrease exceeds the amount of qualified basis with respect to which a credit was allowable for the taxable year referred to in paragraph (1)(B) by reason of subsection (f)(3).

children.heading: No credits against tax
children.label: D No credits against tax
children.text: Any increase in tax under this subsection shall not be treated as a tax imposed by this chapter for purposes of determining the amount of any credit under this chapter.

children.heading: No recapture by reason of casualty loss
children.label: E No recapture by reason of casualty loss
children.text: The increase in tax under this subsection shall not apply to a reduction in qualified basis by reason of a casualty loss to the extent such loss is restored by reconstruction or replacement within a reasonable period established by the Secretary.

children.heading: No recapture where de minimis changes in floor space
children.label: F No recapture where de minimis changes in floor space
i
children.text: such increase results from a de minimis change in the floor space fraction under subsection (c)(1), and

children.label: ii
children.text: the building is a qualified low-income building after such change.

children.heading: Certain partnerships treated as the taxpayer
children.label: 5 Certain partnerships treated as the taxpayer
i
children.text: such partnership shall be treated as the taxpayer to which the credit allowable under subsection (a) was allowed,

children.label: ii
children.text: the amount of such credit allowed shall be treated as the amount which would have been allowed to the partnership were such credit allowable to such partnership,

children.label: iii
children.text: paragraph (4)(A) shall not apply, and

children.label: iv
children.text: the amount of the increase in tax under this subsection for any taxable year shall be allocated among the partners of such partnership in the same manner as such partnership’s taxable income for such year is allocated among such partners.

children.heading: Partnerships to which paragraph applies
children.label: B Partnerships to which paragraph applies
children.text: This paragraph shall apply to any partnership which has 35 or more partners unless the partnership elects not to have this paragraph apply.

children.heading: Husband and wife treated as 1 partner
children.label: i Husband and wife treated as 1 partner
children.text: For purposes of subparagraph (B)(i), a husband and wife (and their estates) shall be treated as 1 partner.

children.heading: Election irrevocable
children.label: ii Election irrevocable
children.text: Any election under subparagraph (B), once made, shall be irrevocable.

children.heading: No recapture on disposition of building which continues in qualified use
children.label: 6 No recapture on disposition of building which continues in qualified use

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The increase in tax under this subsection shall not apply solely by reason of the disposition of a building (or an interest therein) if it is reasonably expected that such building will continue to be operated as a qualified low-income building for the remaining compliance period with respect to such building.

children.label: i
children.text: the statutory period for the assessment of any deficiency with respect to such increase in tax shall not expire before the expiration of 3 years from the date the Secretary is notified by the taxpayer (in such manner as the Secretary may prescribe) of such reduction in qualified basis, and

children.label: ii
children.text: such deficiency may be assessed before the expiration of such 3-year period notwithstanding the provisions of any other law or rule of law which would otherwise prevent such assessment.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Except as otherwise provided in this subsection, rules similar to the rules of section 49(a)(1) (other than subparagraphs (D)(ii)(II) and (D)(iv)(I) thereof), section 49(a)(2), and section 49(b)(1) shall apply in determining the qualified basis of any building in the same manner as such sections apply in determining the credit base of property.

children.heading: Special rules for determining qualified person
children.label: 2 Special rules for determining qualified person
i
children.text: is actively and regularly engaged in the business of lending money, or

children.label: ii
children.text: is a person described in section 49(a)(1)(D)(iv)(II).

children.label: i
children.text: a security interest in such building is not permitted by a Federal agency holding or insuring the mortgage secured by such building, and

children.label: ii
children.text: the proceeds from the financing (if any) are applied to acquire or improve such building.

children.heading: Portion of building attributable to financing
children.label: C Portion of building attributable to financing
children.text: The requirements of this subparagraph are met with respect to any financing for any taxable year in the compliance period if, as of the close of such taxable year, not more than 60 percent of the eligible basis of the qualified low-income building is attributable to such financing (reduced by the principal and interest of any governmental financing which is part of a wrap-around mortgage involving such financing).

children.heading: Repayment of principal and interest
children.label: D Repayment of principal and interest
i
children.text: the date on which such financing matures,

children.label: ii
children.text: the 90th day after the close of the compliance period with respect to the qualified low-income building, or

children.label: iii
children.text: the date of its refinancing or the sale of the building to which such financing relates.

children.heading: Present value of financing
children.label: 3 Present value of financing
children.text: If the rate of interest on any financing described in paragraph (2)(A) is less than the rate which is 1 percentage point below the applicable Federal rate as of the time such financing is incurred, then the qualified basis (to which such financing relates) of the qualified low-income building shall be the present value of the amount of such financing, using as the discount rate such applicable Federal rate. For purposes of the preceding sentence, the rate of interest on any financing shall be determined by treating interest to the extent of government subsidies as not payable.

children.label: i
children.text: beginning with the due date for the filing of the return of tax imposed by chapter 1 for the 1st taxable year for which such credit was allowable, and

children.label: ii
children.text: ending with the due date for the taxable year in which such failure occurs,

children.heading: Applicable portion
children.label: B Applicable portion
children.text: For purposes of subparagraph (A), the term “applicable portion” means the aggregate decrease in the credits allowed to a taxpayer under section 38 for all prior taxable years which would have resulted if the eligible basis of the building were reduced by the amount of financing which does not meet requirements of paragraph (2)(D).

children.heading: Certain rules to apply
children.label: C Certain rules to apply
children.text: Rules similar to the rules of subparagraphs (A) and (D) of subsection (j)(4) shall apply for purposes of this subsection.

children.heading: Certifications and other reports to Secretary
children.label: l Certifications and other reports to Secretary

children.heading: Certification with respect to 1st year of credit period
children.label: 1 Certification with respect to 1st year of credit period
A
children.text: the taxable year, and calendar year, in which such building was placed in service,

children.label: B
children.text: the adjusted basis and eligible basis of such building as of the close of the 1st year of the credit period,

children.label: C
children.text: the maximum applicable percentage and qualified basis permitted to be taken into account by the appropriate housing credit agency under subsection (h),

children.label: D
children.text: the election made under subsection (g) with respect to the qualified low-income housing project of which such building is a part, and

children.label: E
children.text: such other information as the Secretary may require.

children.label: A
children.text: the qualified basis for the taxable year of each qualified low-income building of the taxpayer,

children.label: B
children.text: the information described in paragraph (1)(C) for the taxable year, and

children.label: C
children.text: such other information as the Secretary may require.

children.heading: Annual reports from housing credit agencies
children.label: 3 Annual reports from housing credit agencies
A
children.text: the amount of housing credit amount allocated to each building for such year,

children.label: B
children.text: sufficient information to identify each such building and the taxpayer with respect thereto, and

children.label: C
children.text: such other information as the Secretary may require.

children.heading: Responsibilities of housing credit agencies
children.label: m Responsibilities of housing credit agencies

children.heading: Plans for allocation of credit among projects
children.label: 1 Plans for allocation of credit among projects
i
children.text: such amount was allocated pursuant to a qualified allocation plan of the housing credit agency which is approved by the governmental unit (in accordance with rules similar to the rules of section 147(f)(2) (other than subparagraph (B)(ii) thereof)) of which such agency is a part,

children.label: ii
children.text: such agency notifies the chief executive officer (or the equivalent) of the local jurisdiction within which the building is located of such project and provides such individual a reasonable opportunity to comment on the project,

children.label: iii
children.text: a comprehensive market study of the housing needs of low-income individuals in the area to be served by the project is conducted before the credit allocation is made and at the developer’s expense by a disinterested party who is approved by such agency, and

children.label: iv
children.text: a written explanation is available to the general public for any allocation of a housing credit dollar amount which is not made in accordance with established priorities and selection criteria of the housing credit agency.

children.label: i

children.label: I
children.text: projects serving the lowest income tenants,

children.label: II
children.text: projects obligated to serve qualified tenants for the longest periods, and

children.label: III
children.text: projects which are located in qualified census tracts (as defined in subsection (d)(5)(B)(ii)) and the development of which contributes to a concerted community revitalization plan, and

children.label: iii
children.text: which provides a procedure that the agency (or an agent or other private contractor of such agency) will follow in monitoring for noncompliance with the provisions of this section and in notifying the Internal Revenue Service of such noncompliance which such agency becomes aware of and in monitoring for noncompliance with habitability standards through regular site visits.

children.heading: Certain selection criteria must be used
children.label: C Certain selection criteria must be used
i
children.text: project location,

children.label: ii
children.text: housing needs characteristics,

children.label: iii
children.text: project characteristics, including whether the project includes the use of existing housing as part of a community revitalization plan,

children.label: iv
children.text: sponsor characteristics,

children.label: v
children.text: tenant populations with special housing needs,

children.label: vi
children.text: public housing waiting lists,

children.label: vii
children.text: tenant populations of individuals with children,

children.label: viii
children.text: projects intended for eventual tenant ownership,

children.label: ix
children.text: the energy efficiency of the project, and

children.label: x
children.text: the historic nature of the project.

children.heading: Application to bond financed projects
children.label: D Application to bond financed projects
children.text: Subsection (h)(4) shall not apply to any project unless the project satisfies the requirements for allocation of a housing credit dollar amount under the qualified allocation plan applicable to the area in which the project is located.

children.heading: Credit allocated to building not to exceed amount necessary to assure project feasibility
children.label: 2 Credit allocated to building not to exceed amount necessary to assure project feasibility

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The housing credit dollar amount allocated to a project shall not exceed the amount the housing credit agency determines is necessary for the financial feasibility of the project and its viability as a qualified low-income housing project throughout the credit period.

children.label: i
children.text: the sources and uses of funds and the total financing planned for the project,

children.label: ii
children.text: any proceeds or receipts expected to be generated by reason of tax benefits,

children.label: iii
children.text: the percentage of the housing credit dollar amount used for project costs other than the cost of intermediaries, and

children.label: iv
children.text: the reasonableness of the developmental and operational costs of the project.

children.heading: Determination made when credit amount applied for and when building placed in service
children.label: C Determination made when credit amount applied for and when building placed in service
I
children.text: The application for the housing credit dollar amount.

children.label: II
children.text: The allocation of the housing credit dollar amount.

children.label: III
children.text: The date the building is placed in service.

children.heading: Certification as to amount of other subsidies
children.label: ii Certification as to amount of other subsidies
children.text: Prior to each determination under clause (i), the taxpayer shall certify to the housing credit agency the full extent of all Federal, State, and local subsidies which apply (or which the taxpayer expects to apply) with respect to the building.

children.heading: Application to bond financed projects
children.label: D Application to bond financed projects
children.text: Subsection (h)(4) shall not apply to any project unless the governmental unit which issued the bonds (or on behalf of which the bonds were issued) makes a determination under rules similar to the rules of subparagraphs (A) and (B).

children.label: A
children.text: projects which include more than 1 building or only a portion of a building,

children.label: B
children.text: buildings which are placed in service in portions,
providing for the application of this section to short taxable years,
preventing the avoidance of the rules of this section, and

providing the opportunity for housing credit agencies to correct administrative errors and omissions with respect to allocations and record keeping within a reasonable period after their discovery, taking into account the availability of regulations and other administrative guidance from the Secretary.
